Tests if a variable is an instance of the RegExp object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that creates a regular expression object and verifies its type. The variable `a` is initialized as a new instance of `RegExp`, which is a built-in JavaScript object used for pattern matching within strings. The `assert` function checks that `a` is indeed an instance of `RegExp`, ensuring that the object creation was successful. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on the instantiation and validation of a regular expression object.
